top_signals.title: Minutes of the London FXJSC Main Committee Meeting – 26 March 2026
top_signals.summary: The Bank of England chairs the London Foreign Exchange Joint Standing Committee (FXJSC), which is a forum for discussion of the wholesale foreign exchange market. The FXJSC is made up of market participants, infrastructure providers and the UK financial regulators.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Minutes of the London FXJSC Legal Sub Committee Meeting – 17 March 2026
top_signals.summary: The Bank of England provides the Secretariat to the London Foreign Exchange Joint Standing Committee (FXJSC) Legal Sub-Committee. The FXJSC is made up of market participants, infrastructure providers and the UK financial regulators.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Europe has made a ‘major mistake’ on slow electrification, IEA chief warns
top_signals.summary: The head of the International Energy Agency has warned Europe made a “major mistake” by failing to end its economy reliance on imported fossil fuels quickly enough since the 2022 energy crunch. Fatih Birol said Europe’s low electrification rate, electricity’s share of the energy consumed in the EU, of roughly 23 per cent is holding [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Non-executive director reappointment at GAD
top_signals.summary: The Government Actuary’s Department (GAD) announces the reappointment of Liz Thornhill as non-executive director.
